zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Russell於大約1340年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾時，她的父親John of Bradenstoke11338歲，母親Agnes Creedy22歲。她於1377年與Thomas Quartermain在英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年5月6日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Russell was born about 1340, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John of Bradenstoke, was 11338 and her mother, Agnes Creedy, was 22. She married Thomas Quartermain in 1377, in North Weston, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 6 May 1399, in North Weston, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Russell est née environ 1340, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John of Bradenstoke, avait 11338 ans et sa mère, Agnes Creedy, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Quartermain en 1377, à North Weston, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 mai 1399, à North Weston, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Russellが約1340年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父John of Bradenstokeは11338歳，母Agnes Creedyは22歳であった。1377年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアノース ウエストンでThomas Quartermainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年5月6日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアノース ウエストンで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Russell(이)가 대략 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John of Bradenstoke(은)는 11338세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Creedy(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1377년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 노르스 웨스턴에서 Thomas Quartermain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 5월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 노르스 웨스턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Russell родилась около 1340 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John of Bradenstoke было 11338 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Creedy, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Quartermain в 1377 г. в местности норт вестон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 6 мая 1399 г. в местности норт вестон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет.
